Farm.title: Farm
Farm.description: Represents a physical farm owned by a user, containing multiple fields.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Farm entity.

location.description: Geographical location details of the farm, which can be coordinates or a descriptive address.

ownerId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the user who owns this farm, from an external authentication system. (Relationship: User 1:N Farm)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the farm record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the farm record was last updated.

Field.title: Field
Field.description: Represents an individual agricultural field within a farm, used for tracking crops and soil data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Field entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm this field belongs to. (Relationship: Farm 1:N Field)

name.description: The descriptive name of the field (e.g., 'North Field 1').

areaValue.description: The numerical value of the field's area.

areaUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the field's area (e.g., 'acres', 'hectares').

description.description: An optional description or notes about the field.

lastSoilReportId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the most recent SoilReport for this field, for quick lookup. (Relationship: Field 1:N SoilReport)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the field record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the field record was last updated.

CropCycle.title: CropCycle
CropCycle.description: Tracks the lifecycle of a specific crop planted in a field, including planting, harvesting, and yield.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CropCycle entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field where this crop cycle took place. (Relationship: Field 1:N CropCycle)

cropType.description: The type of crop grown during this cycle (e.g., 'Wheat', 'Corn', 'Soybeans').

plantingDate.description: The date when the crop was planted.

harvestDate.description: The date when the crop was harvested. Can be null if the cycle is ongoing.

actualYieldValue.description: The numerical value of the actual yield obtained from this crop cycle. Can be null if not yet harvested.

actualYieldUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the actual yield (e.g., 'bushels/acre', 'kg/hectare'). Can be null if not yet harvested.

notes.description: Any additional notes or observations about this crop cycle.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the crop cycle record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the crop cycle record was last updated.

SoilReport.title: SoilReport
SoilReport.description: Stores details extracted from a soil analysis report for a specific field.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SoilReport entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field this soil report belongs to. (Relationship: Field 1:N SoilReport)

reportDate.description: The date when the soil sample was taken or the report was generated.

ph.description: The pH level of the soil, indicating its acidity or alkalinity.

nitrogenValue.description: The measured nitrogen content in the soil.

phosphorusValue.description: The measured phosphorus content in the soil.

potassiumValue.description: The measured potassium content in the soil.

moisturePercentage.description: The percentage of moisture content in the soil.

organicMatterPercentage.description: The percentage of organic matter present in the soil.

scanImageUrl.description: URL to the scanned image of the physical soil report, if available.

notes.description: Any additional notes or observations related to the soil report.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the soil report record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the soil report record was last updated.

WeatherObservation.title: WeatherObservation
WeatherObservation.description: Records real-time meteorological data for a specific field location.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WeatherObservation entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field for which this weather observation was recorded. (Relationship: Field 1:N WeatherObservation)

timestamp.description: The exact date and time the weather observation was made.

temperatureCelsius.description: The observed temperature in degrees Celsius.

humidityPercentage.description: The observed relative humidity as a percentage.

precipitationMillimeters.description: The amount of precipitation (e.g., rain, snow) in millimeters during the observation period.

windSpeedMetersPerSecond.description: The observed wind speed in meters per second.

cloudCoverPercentage.description: The percentage of cloud cover observed.

conditionsDescription.description: A brief textual description of the weather conditions (e.g., 'clear sky', 'light rain').

source.description: The source of the weather data (e.g., 'OpenWeather').

WeatherAlert.title: WeatherAlert
WeatherAlert.description: Notifies farmers about critical meteorological conditions affecting their fields.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WeatherAlert entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field to which this weather alert applies. (Relationship: Field 1:N WeatherAlert)

severity.description: The impact level of the alert (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High').

message.description: A detailed message describing the weather alert and its implications.

issuedAt.description: The date and time when the alert was issued.

expiresAt.description: The date and time when the alert is expected to expire. Can be null if ongoing or indefinitely applicable.

isDismissed.description: Indicates whether the user has dismissed this alert.

sourceObservationId.description: Reference to the WeatherObservation that triggered this alert. (Relationship: WeatherObservation 1:N WeatherAlert)

YieldForecast.title: YieldForecast
YieldForecast.description: Stores AI-generated predictions for crop yield for a specific crop cycle.

id.description: Unique identifier for the YieldForecast entity.

cropCycleId.description: Reference to the CropCycle for which this forecast is made. (Relationship: CropCycle 1:N YieldForecast)

forecastDate.description: The date and time when the yield forecast was generated.

predictedYieldValue.description: The numerical value of the predicted yield.

predictedYieldUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the predicted yield (e.g., 'bushels/acre', 'kg/hectare').

confidenceScore.description: A score (0 to 1) indicating the confidence level of the prediction.

explanation.description: An explanation from the Explainable AI Insights describing the factors influencing the forecast.

aiModelVersion.description: The version of the AI model used to generate this forecast.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the yield forecast record was created.

Recommendation.title: Recommendation
Recommendation.description: Provides actionable advice generated by AI for farm management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Recommendation entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field for which this recommendation is provided. (Relationship: Field 1:N Recommendation)

cropCycleId.description: Optional reference to a specific CropCycle if the recommendation is crop-specific. (Relationship: CropCycle 1:N Recommendation)

title.description: A concise title or summary of the recommendation.

description.description: The detailed, actionable advice provided by the AI (e.g., 'Apply 50kg lime/acre').

generatedBy.description: The AI component that generated this recommendation (e.g., 'Generative AI', 'Agri-Bot').

generatedAt.description: The date and time when the recommendation was generated.

isActioned.description: Indicates whether the farmer has taken action on this recommendation.

actionedAt.description: The date and time when the recommendation was actioned. Can be null if not yet actioned.

sourceYieldForecastId.description: Optional reference to the YieldForecast that this recommendation is based on. (Relationship: YieldForecast 1:N Recommendation)

DiseaseDetection.title: DiseaseDetection
DiseaseDetection.description: Records the results of AI vision-based disease and pest detection from uploaded images.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DiseaseDetection entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field where the disease/pest was detected. (Relationship: Field 1:N DiseaseDetection)

cropCycleId.description: Optional reference to the CropCycle during which the detection occurred. (Relationship: CropCycle 1:N DiseaseDetection)

detectionDate.description: The date and time when the disease detection was performed.

imageUrl.description: URL to the image uploaded by the user for detection.

identifiedItems.description: An array of identified pests or diseases from the image, each with its details.

treatmentPlan.description: A multi-part treatment plan provided by the AI for the identified issues.

notes.description: Any additional notes or observations related to the detection or treatment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the disease detection record was created.

definition.description: Represents a farm owned by a user. Each document's 'ownerId' field explicitly identifies the user, aligning with '{userId}' in the path for direct authorization.
Represents an agricultural field within a farm. Documents include denormalized 'ownerId' and 'farmId' fields to ensure authorization independence without requiring parent document lookups.
Tracks the lifecycle of a crop in a field. Documents include denormalized 'ownerId', 'farmId', and 'fieldId' for robust authorization independence.
Stores soil analysis reports for a field. Documents include denormalized 'ownerId', 'farmId', and 'fieldId' for authorization independence.
Records real-time weather data for a field. Documents include denormalized 'ownerId', 'farmId', and 'fieldId' for authorization independence.
Notifies farmers about critical weather conditions. Documents include denormalized 'ownerId', 'farmId', and 'fieldId' for authorization independence.
Stores AI-generated yield predictions for a specific crop cycle. Documents include denormalized 'ownerId', 'farmId', 'fieldId', and 'cropCycleId' for authorization independence.
Provides AI-generated actionable advice for farm management. Documents include denormalized 'ownerId', 'farmId', and 'fieldId' for authorization independence.
Records AI vision-based disease and pest detection results. Documents include denormalized 'ownerId', 'farmId', and 'fieldId' for authorization independence.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for AgriGrow AI is based on a deeply hierarchical, user-centric model, ensuring strict adherence to Authorization Independence and Query-able Access Patterns (QAPs). All data is nested under a `/users/{userId}` root, reflecting the private, user-owned nature of farm management data.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by systematically denormalizing authorization-critical fields into every subcollection document. For instance, a `Field` document, while conceptually belonging to a `Farm`, will explicitly contain its `ownerId` (the user's ID) and `farmId`. Similarly, a `CropCycle` document will include `ownerId`, `farmId`, and `fieldId`. This strategy eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check parent ownership or permissions, making rules atomic, faster, and easier to debug. Operations like creating a new field can be batched with the farm creation or updated independently, without breaking security checks.

**Query-able Access Patterns (QAPs):** By organizing data under `/users/{userId}/...`, `list` queries are inherently secure and efficient. A user can only perform a `list` operation on their own data (e.g., `/users/myUserId/farms`). The security rules can easily enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` for all read/write operations at any level of the hierarchy, preventing unauthorized users from even attempting to query other users' data. Since the path itself filters by user, and denormalized fields confirm ownership, the rules are not used as filters but as strict access enforcers, which is a core QAP principle.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection within the hierarchy maintains a homogeneous security posture: all documents within `/users/{userId}/farms/{farmId}/fields` are private and belong to the specified user and farm. There is no mixing of publicly accessible data with private data in the same collection, simplifying security rules immensely.

**Access Modeling:** The design strictly uses Path-Based ownership and Hierarchical Paths for all user-owned data. This is evident in the nested structure: `users > farms > fields > cropCycles | soilReports | weatherObservations | weatherAlerts | recommendations | diseaseDetections > yieldForecasts`. This consistent pattern is highly predictable and simplifies rule writing to a clear `if request.auth.uid == {userId} && resource.data.ownerId == {userId} && resource.data.parentCollectionId == {parentCollectionId} ...` logic.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:** Wildcards are descriptive (e.g., `{userId}`, `{farmId}`, `{fieldId}`). The schemas include `createdAt` and `updatedAt` for invariants. The explicit denormalization of `ownerId`, `farmId`, `fieldId`, `cropCycleId` (where applicable) further enhances predictability, making it clear which fields are used for authorization at a glance.
